attempts.result: The trust validator did not accept the shell-quoted managed hook command.
Stop committed and the fresh session recalled the record, but the bounded audit preview omitted the marker; live evidence also exposed recommended_plugins scaffolding as writeback input.
The marker-matched Stop record committed after scaffolding filtering, but the fresh query did not recall it.
Persisted injection diagnostics proved the fresh prompt was rejected as low_relevance.
Keyed Stop writeback, fresh-session injection, and recall audit passed; the pre-seeded fact was not injected into the first session.
A separate seed lookup key still failed to select the seeded fact.
A shared lookup key retained the Stop closure, but deterministic trace showed the long first prompt diluted the seeded fact to generic/no-slot selection.
Seed injection, exact-thread Stop hydration and cursor advancement, committed action writeback, distinct-session injection, and recall audit all passed.
Portable rerun passed the same host canary while binding runner-time source identity and safety artifact hashes into the generated tracked projection.
Final portable rerun also retained the source dirty diff artifact, so the tracked projection can verify the runner-time source hash from a copied run bundle.

calibrationDisclosure: The canary prompt was iteratively calibrated with local rules-only BM25 trace after failed runs. The accepted prompt uses one lookup key and withholds both associated values. This calibration is valid for C2 host-chain correctness only and disqualifies C2 from any coding-effect inference.
